title: BioXend pipeline parameters
description: MIX-MB ChEMBL submission file generator — converts a filled MIX-MB template into six ChEMBL-ready deposition files.

input_output_options.title: Input / output
input_output_options.description: Where to find the input template and where to write the output files.

input.description: Path to your filled MIX-MB submission template (.ods).
input.help_text: Download the blank template from `Standards/Templates/Template_open.ods`, fill in all five sheets (Reference, Chemicals, Microbes, Experiment, Biotransformation), and provide the path here. The file must have the `.ods` extension.

outdir.description: Directory where the six ChEMBL deposition files will be written.
outdir.help_text: The pipeline writes REFERENCE.tsv, COMPOUND_RECORD.tsv, COMPOUND_CTAB.sdf, ASSAY.tsv, ASSAY_PARAM.tsv, and ACTIVITY.tsv into this directory. The directory is created automatically if it does not exist.

compound_options.title: Compound options
compound_options.description: Options that control how compound identifiers are generated.

prefix.description: Short alphanumeric prefix for auto-generated compound IDs (CIDXs).
prefix.help_text: Compounds are numbered sequentially from this prefix, e.g. `HMDM` produces `HMDM0001`, `HMDM0002`, etc. Choose a prefix that reflects your study — typically derived from your Reference Identifier (RIDX). Must start with a letter and contain only letters and numbers (2–16 characters).

xenobiotic_class.description: Singular class name for the compounds in this study, used in ASSAY_DESCRIPTION.
xenobiotic_class.help_text: This term appears in the generated assay description text, e.g. 'Biotransformation of [xenobiotic_class] by ...'. Use a singular noun that best describes your compound class. Common values: `drug`, `pesticide`, `food additive`, `environmental contaminant`, `xenobiotic compound`.

pipeline_options.title: Pipeline options
pipeline_options.description: General options that control pipeline behaviour.

strict.description: Fail the pipeline on any validation warning.
strict.help_text: By default the pipeline logs validation warnings and continues. Set `--strict` to treat any warning as a fatal error — recommended before final ChEMBL submission to ensure clean output files.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to publish output files to `--outdir`.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: `copy` (default) writes independent copies of output files. `symlink` creates symbolic links (faster but fragile if the work directory is cleaned). `move` relocates files from the work directory. `link` creates hard links.
